citation: Apel, Mikael and Marianna Blix Grimaldi (2012), "The Information Content of Central Bank Minutes", Sveriges Riksbank Working Paper Series No. 261, April 2012.
notes: Method: two-word noun+adjective combinations (e.g. "higher inflation" = hawkish, "lower growth" = dovish), NOT a single-word sentiment list. The original search was on the Swedish-language Riksbank minutes; the term set here is the paper's own English translation column from its Appendix (p.18), applied to English-language Bank of England minutes text - see DECISIONS.md for the cross-language-application caveat. Terms marked with a trailing '*' in the paper are wildcard stems (matching inflected/compound forms, e.g. inflation* matches 'inflation', 'inflation expectations', 'inflationary'); see DECISIONS.md for how stemming is implemented in this codebase.

nouns_core.page_reference: p.9 (prose) and p.18 (Appendix, English column, wildcard forms)

nouns_extended.notes: Used only for the paper's 'Net Index Extended' robustness check (Table 2, column iv), not the primary Net Index. Not used by this codebase's default index - see DECISIONS.md.

hawkish_adjectives.page_reference: p.9 (prose) and p.18 (Appendix Table, 'Hawkish' English column)

dovish_adjectives.page_reference: p.9 (prose) and p.18 (Appendix Table, 'Dovish' English column)

net_index_formula.notes: #hawk and #dove are the total counts of hawkish and dovish two-word combinations in the document. Range is [0, 2]; 1 is neutral/balanced, confirmed by reading the equation image directly (pdfplumber's text layer does not extract it as text - see DECISIONS.md).

skew_formula.notes: r_j is the rate MPC member j votes for; r is the decided (majority) rate. Used in Part B validation against the Bank's voting record, not in the text-based index itself.
